headline: **MLB Daily Roundup: April 12, 2026**
article:
Shohei Ohtani's leadoff home run sparked a 45-game on-base streak for the Los Angeles Angels, while the Oakland Athletics unveiled an exhilarating, yet intimidating, home run celebration. Meanwhile, the Pittsburgh Pirates capitalized on a key miscue by the Chicago Cubs to secure a gritty series win. José Ramírez became the first Cleveland player to hit a home run against all 29 MLB teams, and Michael Wacha pitched an 8-inning scoreless gem to lower his ERA to 0.43, the best mark in the league. Fernando Tatis Jr. made his first career start at second base and helped lead the San Diego Padres to a win.

Several key players are dealing with injuries, affecting the availability of their teams. The Baltimore Orioles placed catcher Adley Rutschman on the 10-day injured list due to left ankle inflammation. The Tampa Bay Rays have placed right-hander Joe Boyle on the 15-day injured list retroactive to April 10, 2026, due to a right elbow strain. The Atlanta Braves transferred left-hander Danny Young from the 15-day to the 60-day injured list after undergoing left elbow reconstruction. The Minnesota Twins have placed third baseman Royce Lewis on the 10-day injured list retroactive to April 10, 2025, due to a left knee sprain, and right-hander Cody Laweryson on the 15-day injured list retroactive to April 9, 2026, due to a right forearm strain. The Houston Astros placed center fielder Jake Meyers on the 10-day injured list retroactive to April 9, 2026, with a grade two right oblique strain.

The MLB trade market has been active, with several transactions taking place. The Milwaukee Brewers traded right fielder Steward Berroa to the Philadelphia Phillies for cash. The St. Louis Cardinals traded left-hander Nick Raquet to the Baltimore Orioles for second baseman Brayden Smith and acquired right-hander Patrick Galle from the Boston Red Sox. The San Francisco Giants traded second baseman Tyler Fitzgerald to the Toronto Blue Jays for cash. The Los Angeles Angels traded right-hander Victor Mederos to the Atlanta Braves for future considerations and international bonus pool money.

top_news.title: Ohtani's leadoff HR a welcome sight in LA as on-base streak hits 45 games
A's new home run celebration is amazing, but also somewhat terrifying
First-place Bucs capitalize on Cubs' miscue in extras for 'gritty' series win
More history for J-Ram! 3B first Cleveland player with HR vs. other 29 MLB teams
Wacha's 8-inning scoreless gem drops ERA to MLB-best 0.43 mark
Tatis gets first career start at 2B, helps lead Padres to win
Lagrange hits 102+ mph NINE times in latest Triple-A start
Springer undergoing CT scan after fracturing toe on foul
